snapshot.note: Term matches are metadata/name/description/topic heuristics for discovery. They are not authority or support claims.

terms.definition: Unity GameObject/Canvas UI package source and runtime system.
terms.game_ui_use: Use when inspecting Canvas, Graphic, EventSystem, Selectable, ScrollRect, or TMP-in-uGUI source.
terms.not_that: Not UI Toolkit; not NGUI; not a visual style guide.

examples.description: Source code for the Unity UI system.

terms.definition: Unity managed UI system using VisualElement trees, UXML, and USS for editor and runtime UI.
terms.game_ui_use: Use for Panel/UIDocument/VisualElement architecture and runtime or editor UI Toolkit questions.
terms.not_that: Not uGUI Canvas; not archived UIWidgets; not a CSS design system.

examples.description: A first person multiplayer shooter example project in Unity
Streaming server for Unity
Unity ShaderGraph project
Perception toolkit for sim2real training and validation in Unity
Unity UI Toolkit documentation code examples
Repository for the Endless Runner Game Sample (Trash Dash)
This is a sample project to introduce the use of UI Toolkit in Runtime
A package for creating Unity Perception compatible synthetic people.
A set of tools and utilities to help track Unity Editor performance

terms.definition: Historical and namespace lineage name for what is now largely called UI Toolkit in product docs.
terms.game_ui_use: Use when reading older demos, Unite samples, or engine module paths still named UIElements.
terms.not_that: Not a separate current product stack from UI Toolkit; unarchived demos are not automatically current.

examples.description: Unity project containing examples to use UIElements in the Editor
This is the project used in the Unite Copenhagen 2019 presentation: Building UI for games with the new UI Builder
Demo Used in Unite LA 2018 UIElements talk.

terms.definition: Runtime or editor attachment of a VisualTreeAsset-derived VisualElement tree to Panel Settings.
terms.game_ui_use: Open when mapping screen hierarchy to UI Toolkit document ownership.
terms.not_that: Not a Canvas; VisualElement children are not Transform children.

terms.definition: Node type in the UI Toolkit visual tree; layout, style, and focus operate on this tree.
terms.game_ui_use: Use when tracing hierarchy, layout, focus, or binding ownership in UI Toolkit.
terms.not_that: Not a GameObject/RectTransform; not NGUI UIWidget.

terms.definition: XML-like markup for UI Toolkit visual trees.
terms.game_ui_use: Use when locating document structure and control composition examples.
terms.not_that: Not uGUI prefab hierarchy; path hits alone do not prove current support.

terms.definition: Style sheet language for UI Toolkit presentation and some transitions.
terms.game_ui_use: Use when studying style resolution and visual presentation separation from layout ownership.
terms.not_that: Not reusable StyleGallery Layout CSS; not proof of product visual defaults.

terms.definition: uGUI rendering and hierarchy host for Graphic components in the Game View.
terms.game_ui_use: Use for uGUI screen layering, rebuild, and raycast questions.
terms.not_that: Not UI Toolkit Panel; Canvas membership is runtime state.

terms.definition: uGUI input routing host that selects and runs an active BaseInputModule.
terms.game_ui_use: Use when verifying click, drag, select, and navigation ownership for Canvas UI.
terms.not_that: Not NGUI UICamera; not by itself UI Toolkit Panel focus.

terms.definition: Package for devices, actions, and UI integration modules such as InputSystemUIInputModule.
terms.game_ui_use: Use when UI input, gamepad-as-cursor, or action-map integration is the claim.
terms.not_that: Not the layout system; not complete UI Toolkit input story by itself.

examples.description: An efficient and versatile input system for Unity.

Bagel Game is a full vertical game project that is specifically made to demonstrate how UI Toolkit can be used to create UI for 3D games. It covers World Space UI, Custom Shaders, Filters, Data Bindings, StyleSheets, Themes, Input System Integration, among others - all in a realistic fully functional game project.
Input prototype revealed here: https://blogs.unity3d.com/2016/04/12/developing-the-new-input-system-together-with-you/

terms.definition: Text stack currently sourced under the uGUI package tree rather than a standalone org repo in this snapshot.
terms.game_ui_use: Use uGUI TMP paths and package manuals for text claims.
terms.not_that: No standalone TextMeshPro org source repo in the 804-row inventory.

terms.definition: Locale, string table, and language-delivery concerns usually package- and sample-driven.
terms.game_ui_use: Treat as cross-cutting evidence; package docs may be docs-only without a dedicated org source mirror.
terms.not_that: Zero primary_cluster rows for localization-text in the exclusive taxonomy; absence of cluster rows is not proof of absence.

terms.definition: Assistive technology, screen reader, and focus-order evidence layer.
terms.game_ui_use: Use a11y samples and manuals with version pins; do not infer accessibility from UI stack choice alone.
terms.not_that: Focus order docs are narrower than verified AT outcomes.

examples.description: Sample project using the Unity Accessibility APIs

terms.definition: Separate Flutter-derived UI framework historically published under Unity-Technologies and later archived/redirected.
terms.game_ui_use: Historical or China-lineage research only.
terms.not_that: Not UI Toolkit; not current general Game UI authority.

examples.description: UIWidgets is a Unity Package which helps developers to create, debug and deploy efficient, cross-platform Apps.

terms.definition: Third-party GameObject UI stack owned outside Unity-Technologies.
terms.game_ui_use: Compare input/hierarchy/migration only via separately pinned tasharen/ngui evidence.
terms.not_that: Never counted inside the 804 Unity-Technologies inventory.

terms.definition: UI Toolkit host for a VisualElement tree, repaint, focus, and event dispatch.
terms.game_ui_use: Use UnityCsReference Modules/UIElements Panel paths for architecture claims.
terms.not_that: Not uGUI Canvas; pin Unity version branch.

terms.definition: Immediate-mode GUI used heavily in editor tooling and legacy runtime OnGUI paths.
terms.game_ui_use: Use for editor tool UI and compare-table context only.
terms.not_that: Input System is not generally compatible with runtime IMGUI under input-system-only player settings.

terms.definition: Content delivery and async asset loading package/samples that often host UI asset lifecycle.
terms.game_ui_use: Open when UI load/unload, remote content, or async screen state depends on addressable groups.
terms.not_that: Not a UI system; sample HUD is observation only.

examples.description: Demo project using Addressables package
Unite Seoul 2025 - Addressables: Best Practices

terms.definition: Data-oriented ECS/DOTS runtime and samples, including some UI Toolkit observations.
terms.game_ui_use: Use for state ownership and performance context that constrains UI update flow.
terms.not_that: DotsUI/samples are observations, not the general UI contract.

examples.description: Samples designed as exercises to be ported from Unity GameObjects/MonoBehaviours to Unity DOTS.

Megacity-Metro: a thrilling shooter game, using Netcode for Entities for a multiplayer experience supporting 128+ players. Latest DOTS packages and Unity Gaming Services elevate the user experience, demonstrating how to craft engaging multiplayer games.
A third person, multiplayer sample project. Built with Unity and using the new Data Oriented Tech Stack (DOTS).
ECS multiplayer racing sample to showcase using Unity Entities and netcode with best practices

Megacity is a sample showcasing streaming a large scale environment. It also contains an action-packed, multiplayer shooter mode. It leverages the power of Netcode for Entities for an immersive, multiplayer experience that can support 64+ players simultaneously.
Code samples using the animation C# jobs feature.
Repository of projects that showcase the new DOTS animation package (com.unity.animation).
Sample projects for the Unity.CharacterController package
ECS sample demonstrating an automated simulation of teams of spaceships fighting for the control of planets

terms.definition: HPC# compiler/runtime used with Jobs/Entities workloads.
terms.game_ui_use: Relevant only as runtime constraint context near ECS UI observations.
terms.not_that: Not a UI framework.

examples.description: Unity blog-post sample showing BatchRendererGroup and Burst/JobSystem. Focus is high performance even on budget mobile devices. Unity 2022.3.5 or above required
